titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jesse Deane Papers and Photographs
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday March 25, 2020 at 03:42

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 30-37 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bolerium Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Photographs, circa 1940s-2000s. II. Audiovisual, 1958-1992. III. Correspondence, 1951 - 2009. IV. Personal papers, 1961-2001. V. Printed material, 1950 - 2013.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jesse Deane papers and photographs. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Jesse Deane papers and photographs contains correspondence, photographs, negatives, film, videocassettes, printed material, personal papers, artwork and other material by or relating to physique model, photographer, and gay pornographic actor Jesse Deane. Of note is a photographic album and slides that document Fire Island, N.Y. in the 1960s.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jesse Dean papers and photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Jesse Deane
bioghist.p: Jesse Deane (born Richmond, Virginia, 1938) was born Robert Allen Reed and grew up in Starksville, Missouri. Deane was a physique model, photographer, and gay pornographic actor. Deane studied acting at the James Dean Memorial School of Fine Arts in Fairmont, Indiana before moving to New York City, where from 1956-1960 he appeared in off-Broadways productions, including a role in Eddie Fay , directed by Nils L. Cruz at the New Village Theatre. In 1965, he was both a photographer and model for Butch magazine, an early gay publication. Deane starred in the 1970 film Sticks and Stones , which provides a representation of gay culture on Fire Island in the 1960s. Deane died on January 6, 2014.

subject.part: Gay community -- New York (State) -- Fire Island (Island)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes photographic prints, slides, and negatives.

Personal photographs depict Deane's friends, family, and pets, as well as many images of Deane. Personal photographs also document Deane's travels to New York City, his time living in San Francisco, and attendance at Gay Pride Parades. Also included is an album of photographs, as well as slides, that Deane took on Fire Island, N.Y.
Professional photographs relate to Deane's career as a pornographic actor and physique model, and includes images of other models.
Photographs of Jesse Deane include nude modeling and pornographic images of Deane.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this series is arranged by subject, and further arranged chronologically. Negatives and slides are located at the end of the series.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Some photographs and negatives have adhered together. Use caution while handling and do not attempt to separate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negatives document personal and family photographs, and professional images including Deane's physique modelling and pornographic images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slides depict pornographic images and physique models. Images also include travel and other personal photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this series is arranged by format.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains 8mm film reels and videocassettes that include commercial films that Deane starred in, such as Sticks and Stones , as well as non-commercial films.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies of film may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes hand labelled titles, "Just F.I.," "David Carter," "Bob Reed c/o James Dean Foundation," and "Waverly." Commercial titles include "Laurel and Hardy," and "Lawrence of Arabia." Untitled films are also included.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies of film may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes hand labeled titles, "Cowboy," and "Young Blood Parts 1 + 2." Untitled films are also included.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies of film may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes hand labeled title, "Home with Mom." Untitled films are also included.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies of film may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: This videocassette is commercial. It has not been digitized.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: This videocassette is commercial. It has not been digitized.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this series is arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal correspondence including greeting cards, postcards, and letters. Also includes professional correspondence related to Deane's career as a model.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Personal papers, 1961 - 2001. Objects, undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains personal papers including artwork, financial records, military records, and movie scripts. Objects in this series include a stamp from Cherry Grove, Fire Island, and promotional material fom Dick, A Film by Jo Menell.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains Deanes' personal papers, including artwork, financial records, military records, movie scripts, notes, and writings.

did.unittitle: The Concorde-Airport '79 cast and crew list

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material in this subseries contains ephemera, including a stamp from Cherry Grove, Fire Island, and promotional material from Dick, A Film by Jo Menell.

did.unittitle: Hollywood Walk of Fame "Jesse Will Deane" novelty sign
Stamp: "The Rack Cherry Grove, Fire Island, N.Y."
Dick: A Film by Jo Menell promotional calendar and t-shirt

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material in this series is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains clippings, ephemera, playbills, travel brochures, and newspaper publications.

did.unittitle: Pornographic publications: The Boys in the Band, Grecian Guild Pictorial, and Male Nudist Review
Publications: Pageant, Playbill, and Writer's Digest
Queer Baby Names by Matthew Rettenmund and Jaye Zimet
